name: Circular Route of the Battlefields of La Albuera
name_original: Rivera de Limonetes — circular desde La Albuera
description: Hike the 12.7-mile Circular Route of the Battlefields of La Albuera, a challenging trail through historic Spanish landscapes.
region_intro: Hiking around Badajoz offers diverse landscapes, from rugged mountains and rolling hills to ancient forests and river valleys. The region is characterized by expansive oak dehesas, granite boulders, and varied vegetation including holm oaks and chestnut trees. Hikers can explore areas with significant megalithic culture and historical features like Roman dams. Best hiking trails around Badajoz The most popular hiking route is Route of the XII Apostles — circular from Salvaleón, a 9.97 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This route traverses rolling hills and dehesa landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Circular Route of the Battlefields of La Albuera, a difficult 12.72 miles (20.5 km) path. This trail offers a longer, more challenging experience through historical terrain. Local hikers also love the Dolmen La Hermosina loop from Barcarrota, a 3.83 miles (6.2 km) trail leading through areas with megalithic elements, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Hiking around Badajoz is defined by oak dehesas, rolling hills, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to difficult long-distance routes. The routes in Badajoz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 60 hikers have used komoot to explore Badajoz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Badajoz?
A: Badajoz offers a variety of hiking experiences with over 20 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging long-distance paths, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Badajoz?
A: Hikers in Badajoz will encounter diverse landscapes, including expansive oak &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, rolling hills, and river valleys. Notable areas include the gentle mountains of Cornalvo Natural Park, the higher peaks of Sierra de Tentudía, and the biodiverse region of La Siberia. You'll also find granite boulders, ancient forests, and historical features like Roman dams and megalithic dolmens.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Badajoz?
A: Yes, Badajoz has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31397567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen La Hermosina loop from Barcarrota&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.2 km route that takes about 1 hour 39 minutes, leading through areas with megalithic elements. Cornalvo Natural Park also offers easy circular routes, such as a 7.5 km path featuring the Roman Cornalvo Dam.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Badajoz?
A: Many trails in Badajoz are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219153536/route-of-the-xii-apostles-circular-from-salvaleon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the XII Apostles — circular from Salvaleón&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 16.1 km trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991621788/castillo-de-nogales-circular-by-los-ejidos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Nogales — circular by Los Ejidos&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.7 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222605650/nogales-castle-and-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle and Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 14.8 km route.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails in Badajoz?
A: Hiking in Badajoz offers a chance to explore significant historical and natural attractions. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6249042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle&lt;/a&gt;, explore areas with megalithic dolmens like those near Barcarrota, or see the Roman Cornalvo Dam in Cornalvo Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6248933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Battle of Albuera Memorial Mosaic&lt;/a&gt; is another notable site, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3272177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Twelve Apostles (Centenary Cork Oaks)&lt;/a&gt; highlights ancient trees.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Badajoz?
A: The best time for hiking in Badajoz is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant, with blooming wildflowers in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be wet.

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance hikes in Badajoz?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Badajoz offers more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222650884/circular-route-of-the-battlefields-of-la-albuera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route of the Battlefields of La Albuera&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 20.5 km path. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219167772/route-of-the-xii-apostles-circular-from-nogales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the XII Apostles — circular from Nogales&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 20.2 km trail.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Badajoz?
A: The hiking trails in Badajoz are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse terrain, the beauty of the oak &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, and the historical elements found along the routes.

Q: Can I find trails that offer panoramic views in Badajoz?
A: Yes, several areas in Badajoz provide excellent viewpoints. The Sierra de Alor offers panoramic views of the Llanos de Olivenza region. Additionally, the Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail (SL-BA 198) is a moderate 4.6 km route known for its panoramic views of the La Serena reservoir.

Q: Are there specific natural parks or protected areas for hiking in Badajoz?
A: Badajoz is home to significant natural areas ideal for hiking. The Cornalvo Natural Park, near Mérida, features gentle mountains, rivers, and expansive oak &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;. The Sierra de Tentudía, in the south, is a Special Conservation Area and boasts Cerro de Tentudía, the highest peak in Badajoz, offering diverse vegetation and wildlife.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the trails in Badajoz?
A: The terrain in Badajoz is varied. You'll encounter rolling hills, flat sections through &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; (oak woodlands), and some rugged mountainous paths, particularly in areas like the Sierra de Tentudía. Many trails feature natural surfaces, including dirt paths, rocky sections, and sometimes gravel tracks.
tips: In 1811, in the War of Independence, the famous battle of La Albuera took place. As a reminder of that battle, you can see, on Avenida de Extremadura, two large mosaics commemorating it, which was the beginning of the end of the French invasion of Spanish territory.
It is part of the ZEC «Rivera de los Limonetes-Nogales». We can observe the native vegetation, a very scenic place along the banks of the river.
Together with Ribera de Nogales, it is part of the ZEC «Rivera de los Limonetes-Nogales». We can observe the native vegetation and enjoy nature.

faqs.question: What is the starting point and parking situation for this route?
faqs.answer: The Circular Route of the Battlefields of La Albuera begins and ends on Avenida de Extremadura in the municipality of La Albuera. Parking information is not explicitly provided in the available data, but as it starts in the town, street parking or designated public parking areas within La Albuera would be the most likely options.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Circular Route of the Battlefields of La Albuera?
faqs.answer: The trail is notably flat and traverses expansive, open fields, offering unobstructed views of the Spanish landscape. You will cross the Albuera River via two bridges. While generally flat, the terrain immediately to the left of a smaller, older bridge over the river is described as abrupt and uneven. The route also features areas with olive groves.

faqs.question: How challenging is this hike?
faqs.answer: Despite being rated as 'difficult' in some assessments, the trail is described as 'eminently flat' for the most part, suggesting a low difficulty level suitable for most hikers. The difficulty rating likely stems from its length of over 20 km rather than significant elevation changes or technical terrain.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route takes you across the historic grounds of the Battle of La Albuera. While the battlefield itself is largely open space today, you can visit the Battle of Albuera Memorial Mosaic in town. The trail allows you to follow the historical path of the French forces' approach and observe the existing bridge over the Albuera River, which played a role in the battle. Memorials in La Albuera town, including a grand white stone arch and a monument unveiled by the Duke of Wellington, commemorate the event.

faqs.question: Is the Circular Route of the Battlefields of La Albuera dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The available information does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed on this specific trail. As a general rule for outdoor routes in Spain, dogs are often permitted but should be kept on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. It's advisable to check local regulations or signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The data does not specify the best time of year. Given its location in Extremadura, Spain, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and potentially wet, though the flat terrain would likely remain accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No permits or entrance fees are mentioned in the provided information for hiking the Circular Route of the Battlefields of La Albuera. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: What natural features will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: You will encounter the Albuera River, crossing it via two bridges. The route also passes by the Riverside of Nogales and the Chicaspiernas River. The landscape is characterized by expansive open fields and olive groves.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby after the hike?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in the municipality of La Albuera. As a town, La Albuera would likely offer local amenities such as cafes and restaurants where you can refresh yourself after completing the hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While the terrain is largely flat and easy, the route is over 20 km long. This significant distance might be challenging for younger children or those not accustomed to long walks. Families with older, experienced hikers might find it suitable, but its length makes it less ideal for very young children.

faqs.question: Are there any alternative hiking routes in the La Albuera area?
faqs.answer: The provided information focuses specifically on the 'Circular Route of the Battlefields of La Albuera'. While other routes may exist in the broader region, details on specific alternatives are not available in the given data.
